steps.title: Find the intercept value
steps.content:
The y-intercept occurs when $x=0$. Substitute $0$ into the original function so the value can be compared with each rewritten form.
$$f(0)=a(2.4^0+2.4^b)$$
$$f(0)=a(1+2.4^b)$$

steps.title: Check form one
steps.content: For $g(x)=a(2.4^x+k)$ to match $f$, $k=2.4^b$. The displayed constants or coefficients are $a$ and $k$, but the intercept is $a(1+k)$, not either one by itself.

steps.title: Check form two
steps.content: For $h(x)=a(2.4)^x+m$ to match $f$, $m=a\cdot2.4^b$. The intercept is $a+m$, not $a$ or $m$ alone.

steps.title: Choose neither form
steps.content:
Neither form displays the y-intercept as a single constant or coefficient.
The answer is D.
